text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Goolsbee says inflation still too high, Williams sees it easing — that tug-of-war is keeping rate-cut hopes uncertain, which is exactly what pressures high-multiple tech and chip names. S&P headline warns of a critical crossroads, so sentiment is fragile.

Your holdings: PLTR is the most urgent — down 17.56% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast, and it has the furthest to fall before your $85 stop. AVGO is your largest position, down 13.18% with a TRIM signal — concentration plus margin here is a real risk if the tape rolls over. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — tiny cushion to your $150 stop, and margin amplifies any gap-down.

Watch: (1) Whether SPY breaks down through near-term support — headline flags that risk explicitly. (2) Any Fed speaker follow-up on inflation, since that's the direct lever on your chip and growth names.

Bottom line: PLTR and AVGO demand action now — margin means you can't afford to let SELL and TRIM signals sit.
